<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
before_ascii = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
after_ascii = last_transition.after_frame.ascii.split('\n')

changed_cells = []
for r in range(64):
    for c in range(64):
        if r < len(before_ascii) and c < len(before_ascii[r]):
            if r < len(after_ascii) and c < len(after_ascii[r]):
                if before_ascii[r][c] != after_ascii[r][c]:
                    changed_cells.append((r, c, before_ascii[r][c], after_ascii[r][c]))

print("Changed cells:", changed_cells)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Only the HUD bar changed again. So clicking the r dot at (10, 31) is still just a wasted move.
Let me reconsider the puzzle. I've been clicking on:
1. r dot at (10, 31) - no gameplay change (HUD only)
2. B center of cross at (10, 52) - no gameplay change (HUD only)
3. N shape in big container at (21, 39) - caused small green container N to shrink
4. Other N shape in big container at (21, 45) - caused small green container N to grow back
5. r dot at (10, 31) again - no gameplay change (HUD only)
So the only actions that cause gameplay changes are clicking on the N shapes inside the big container. But those just toggle the small container's N shape between two states.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal isn't to empty the small containers but to change the shapes in the big containers. Let me look more carefully at the big container's compartments.
The big container (top-right) has two compartments:
- Left: N shape (7 pixels, C/U shape)
- Right: N shape (7 pixels, same C/U shape)
The small green container has a 3x3 filled N square that toggles between full and partial.
Maybe the goal is to make the big container shapes match the small container shape? Or maybe I need to click on the compartment walls (the c-colored walls) to change the shape inside?
Let me try clicking on the c-colored wall of one of the compartments in the big container.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider. The small container's N shape toggled between:
1. Full 3x3 (9 pixels) with r dot at center (10, 31)
2. Partial (5 pixels) with r dot at left edge (10, 28)
And this was triggered by clicking on the N shapes in the big container. But the big container shapes never changed.
What if the small container is a "palette" or "color picker" and I need to select a color/shape from it, then click on the big container to apply it?
Or what if I need to click on the r dot in the small container FIRST to "select" the N color, then click on the big container?
But I already tried clicking the r dot and it did nothing...
Let me try a different approach. Let me look at the yellow bar on the left and the Y shapes in the bottom container. Maybe the pattern is different there.
Let me click on a Y shape in the bottom container to see what happens.
